finding: STOP/Target mix with MAX_HOLD appearing only via rolling-low path
interpretation: Rolling stops widen path → longer bars → MAX_HOLD usage; signal_low combos show STOP/TARGET only.
notes: Pooled totals sum across combos; per-combo JSON on each sweep row resolves ambiguity.

finding: Rolling combos average fewer pure STOP exits vs signal_low (~35 vs ~67 STOP counts per combo)
supporting_metric: Per-row JSON aggregated in Phase 6d python helper
interpretation: Rolling structural stop reallocates exits across STOP/TARGET/MAX_HOLD vs tight bar lows.
notes: Counts are exploratory not risk-normalized here.

finding: Reject reasons empty aggregate; per-row `{}` rejects
interpretation: No malformed-intent rejects surfaced in artifacts.
notes: Does not guarantee absence of rejects in future windows.

interpretation: Consistent with `max_trades_per_session: 1`; high skip volume expected.
notes: Enumerates exploratory opportunities lost to policy not errors.

interpretation: Skipping while flat open competes strongly with trade cap—not a pathology by itself.

finding: trade_open skips vary materially by combo
interpretation: different VWAP tightening & stop interplay changes concurrency of valid intents
notes: Indicates apples-to-apples comparisons should fix scan policy identical (they are).

finding: Aggregate distro CSV merges across combos—not ideal for adjudicating one row
interpretation: Selections should reference per-row JSON fields already stored in CSV.
notes: Blocks naive ranking on pooled exit charts only—not selection design entirely.

finding: target_r adjusts STOP/TARGET balance within signal_low
supporting_metric: Paired inspection of combos 0001 vs 0002 etc.
interpretation: Higher target tends to ↑STOP / ↓TARGET on signal path; interacts with economics already negative.

finding: Rolling lowers average STOP exits and activates MAX_HOLD
interpretation: Mechanical consequence of farther stop placement + max-hold horizon.
notes: Aligns with longer avg bars held metrics in sweep.
